_note: visualDescription for every photography project: short comma-separated fragments of one, two or three words (no uniform pattern, not sentences), mixing material/color/what-it-resembles with the scientific fact that grounds it for a non-seeing reader (e.g. gypsum=the mineral in plaster; Simpson=the red centre of Australia, iron-red; Greenland=glacial ice & meltwater; Palouse=loess/wind-blown silt). winterblue/flipped/palouse carry this in their own files; merge surface-surveys + 6 chapters + omani-landscapes into data/ssot-projects.json as top-level `visualDescription`, rendered as image alt/longdesc. Aerial vs ground: Greenland & Simpson = aerial; Wahiba & City of Rocks = ground; White Sands & Craters = both; scale ambiguity is intentional. Field/documentary/scouting images (people, vehicles, camps) are NOT artworks and are excluded.

visualDescription.surface-surveys: ice, sand, lava, gypsum, granite, red dune, elemental, ambiguous scale
visualDescription.greenland: glacial ice, meltwater, turquoise, cobalt, crevasses, aerial
visualDescription.white-sands: gypsum, plaster, blinding white, dripping plaster, meringue peaks, close or aerial
visualDescription.wahiba: Wahiba Sands, honey-gold, quartz grains, wind ripples, scribed circles
visualDescription.craters: basalt, cooled lava, black, rust-red, gold lichen, lime lichen, iron stain, aerial or close
visualDescription.simpson: red centre, Australia, iron-red, oxblood, trailing vines, aerial
visualDescription.city-of-rocks: granite, salt-and-pepper grey, rust-orange, acid-yellow lichen, pockmarked
visualDescription.omani-landscapes: pink, green, feminine, bread browns, earth tones, dissolved landform, form versus function
visualDescription.winterblue: midnight blue, indigo, ice blue, monochromatic, snow, wind-carved drifts, moonlight, sub-zero
visualDescription.flipped: cinematic, monumental, icebergs, luminous blue-white, looming peaks, black mirror water, backlit, moody, otherworldly, inverted
visualDescription.palouse: loess hills, wind-blown silt, rolling, plow-line corduroy, spare
